title: Guitar Chord Library
description: A complete guitar chord library for the Chord Flash Cards feature. Contains a difficulty-tier registry, a chord-quality registry (interval formulas), and the chord records themselves (each with one or more verified voicings). Conventions: all 6-element arrays are ordered from string 6 (low E, index 0) to string 1 (high E, index 5). Interval tokens and note names use ASCII accidentals ('b' = flat, '#' = sharp, 'bb'/'##' = double). The UI may render these as unicode.

schemaVersion.description: Semantic version of this library file's structure, e.g. '1.0.0'.

tuning.description: Scientific-pitch tuning of each string from low (string 6) to high (string 1). Standard tuning shown in the example.

fretConvention.description: Human-readable reminder of the frets encoding. See the 'frets' property of a voicing for the authoritative definition.

tiers.description: Difficulty tiers, ordered easiest-first. See the taxonomy doc for full contents.

qualities.description: Chord-quality registry. One entry per quality (major, m7, dim7, ...). Holds the canonical interval formula and symbols; chord records reference these by id. Deriving spellings for all 12 roots from these formulas is safe, deterministic theory.

chords.description: The chord records. Typically one per (root x quality) combination the library covers, each carrying one or more verified voicings.

noteName.description: A pitch class with optional accidental, e.g. 'C', 'F#', 'Bb', 'G##'. No octave.

intervalToken.description: A chord-tone degree relative to the root's major scale, with optional accidental(s). Examples: '1', '3', 'b3', '5', 'b5', '#5', 'b7', '7', '9', 'b9', '#9', '11', '#11', '13', 'b13', 'bb7'.

cagedShape.description: The CAGED parent shape of a movable voicing, or null if not applicable.

category.description: The shape family a voicing belongs to. Drives difficulty and how the app teaches it.

family.description: Broad grouping of a chord quality.

prerequisiteTier.description: order of the tier that should be mastered first, or null for the first tier.

qualityIds.description: Ids of the qualities that debut in this tier.

id.description: Stable id, e.g. 'maj7', 'm7b5', '7sharp9'. Used by chord.quality.

symbol.description: Canonical display symbol appended to the root, e.g. 'maj7', 'm7', '5', 'dim7'. Empty string for a plain major triad.

aliases.description: Alternate display symbols, e.g. ['M7', 'Δ'] for maj7.

formula.description: Root-relative interval tokens defining the quality, in ascending order.

difficultyTier.description: Tier order where this quality first appears in its easiest voicing.

sourceSuffix.description: Optional: the suffix key in the source voicing dataset (e.g. chords-db) this quality maps to, to ease ingestion.

quality.description: Id of a quality in the qualities registry.

formula.description: Resolved interval tokens for this chord (usually identical to the quality's formula; carried here for convenience).

spelling.description: The actual note names produced by applying the formula to the root, e.g. ['C','E','G','B'] for Cmaj7. Derivable deterministically from root + formula.

difficultyTier.description: Tier of this chord's easiest voicing.

voicings.description: One or more playable shapes for this chord. Exactly one should have recommendedForTier=true per difficulty tier the chord is taught at.

label.description: Human label shown in the reveal carousel, e.g. 'A-shape barre, 3rd fret'.

frets.description: Fret per string, ordered string 6 (low E) -> string 1 (high E). Value -1 = muted, 0 = open, N>=1 = the Nth fret WITHIN the diagram window, where 1 is the baseFret itself. Absolute fret = baseFret + value - 1 (for value >= 1). This matches the chords-db convention, so source data ingests directly.

fingers.description: Fretting finger per string, same ordering as frets. 0 = open or muted (no finger), 1 = index, 2 = middle, 3 = ring, 4 = pinky.

baseFret.description: Lowest fret shown in the diagram window. 1 means the window includes the nut (open position).

barres.description: Barre(s) in this voicing. Empty or omitted if none.

capo.description: Optional rendering hint: draw the barre as a capo bar. Convenience carried from source datasets.

inversionBass.description: For inversions / slash chords: the note in the bass, e.g. 'G' for C/G. Null for root-position voicings.

midi.description: Optional: sounding MIDI note numbers, low to high, for playback and Phase-2 audio checking. Length equals the number of non-muted strings.

difficultyTier.description: Difficulty of THIS voicing (may exceed the chord's own difficultyTier).

recommendedForTier.description: True if this is the voicing to show first for a student at this voicing's tier. Prevents overwhelming beginners with advanced grips.

fret.description: Fret of the barre, expressed the same window-relative way as the frets array (1 = baseFret).

fromString.description: Lowest string index covered (0 = low E).

toString.description: Highest string index covered (5 = high E).
